mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal robust consumer spending and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings and yields positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum loses predictive power for 5-day SPX returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, with out-of-sample accuracy falling below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level crossing above 330 reflects entrenched inflation that forces aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns remain positive in greater than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 330, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while retail sales accelerate, it signals a shift toward services consumption that boosts equity earnings expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index crossing above 102 indicates capacity utilization approaching peak levels, triggering supply constraints and negative short-term equity returns due to margin pressure.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 102, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 51%.
